layers.name: Godot MIDI Player
layers.description: Core MIDI playback engine: SF2 synthesis, voice pooling, channel state, and audio stream scheduling

layers.name: Godot Editor Tools
layers.description: Editor-only tools: piano roll, transport bar, MIDI monitor, soundfont browser, mini mixer, and Clef Station

layers.name: Godot Core Data
layers.description: Core data types (MidiData, NoteData, TrackData), MIDI reader/writer, converter, resource classes, import/inspector plugins, player UI, and plugin entry point

layers.name: Godot Tests
layers.description: GDScript test scripts for MIDI player, reader, and CC/pitchbend roundtrip verification

layers.name: LLM Composition Scripts
layers.description: Python scripts for ABC-to-MIDI conversion, validation, merging, expression injection, solo extraction, agent call logging, and the unified CLI entry point

layers.name: LLM Composition Tests
layers.description: Python pytest test suite for composition scripts including ABC conversion, MIDI analysis, validation, pipeline, and E2E tests

layers.name: LLM Agent Definitions
layers.description: Agent prompt files and skill definitions for the multi-agent composition system: composer, harmonist, rhythmist, orchestrator, reviewer, revision, and leader

layers.name: Documentation
layers.description: Project README files, plugin user guide, JSON spec docs, knowledge README, and design/plan documents

layers.name: Configuration
layers.description: Project configuration files: Godot project settings, plugin config, LLM templates, SF2 profiles, test fixtures, and composition schemas

tour.title: Project Overview
tour.description: Start with the project README to understand Clef's mission: a Godot 4.6+ MIDI playback engine with SF2 synthesis and a multi-agent LLM composition system. This establishes the two-pillar architecture before diving into code.

tour.title: Godot Plugin Entry Point
tour.description: Examine the Godot project configuration and plugin registration. project.godot defines autoloads and the main scene; plugin.cfg declares the Clef addon; plugin.gd (highest fan-in at 11 references) is the EditorPlugin that bootstraps the entire MIDI system in the Godot editor.

tour.title: MIDI Data Types & Resources
tour.description: The data foundation layer. MidiData, NoteData, and TrackData define the in-memory representation of MIDI content. MidiResource serializes this into Godot's Resource system so MIDI files can be imported and used like any other asset.

tour.title: MIDI Stream Player — The Core Engine
tour.description: The heart of the playback system. MidiStreamPlayer implements a tick-based sequencer that schedules note-on/note-off events onto an AudioStreamPlayer pool. It handles tempo changes, program changes, CC messages, and pitch bend in real time. Understanding this file is essential for the entire audio pipeline.

tour.title: SF2 SoundFont Synthesis
tour.description: How sound is actually produced. Sf2Reader parses SF2 files to extract presets and samples. ClefBank provides the high-level instrument abstraction, and ClefVoice is the single-note synthesis unit that wraps AudioStreamPlayer with pitch_scale for tuning and volume_db for ADSR envelope.

tour.title: MIDI I/O Pipeline
tour.description: The data conversion layer that bridges external formats with the internal MidiData model. Converter translates JSON scene data (v2 beat-unit format) into MidiData. MidiReader parses binary .mid files. MidiWriter exports MidiData back to standard MIDI binary. Together they form the complete round-trip pipeline.

tour.title: Editor Tools — Clef Station
tour.description: The in-editor MIDI workstation. ClefStation is the main dock panel that orchestrates the editor player, MIDI monitor, soundfont browser, piano roll, and mini mixer. These tools let you audition MIDI files, browse GM patches, and inspect real-time playback events without leaving the Godot editor.

tour.title: LLM Composition System — The Skill
tour.description: The orchestration entry point for AI-assisted composition. SKILL.md (highest fan-out at 18 references) defines the /clef-compose command workflow: Step 0 requirements parsing, Step 1 plan + direction sample, Step 2 full creation with agent iteration, Step 3 expression injection. theory.md provides the music theory reference used by all agents.

tour.title: Agent Team — Creative & Quality
tour.description: The seven specialized agents that collaborate on composition. The Composer (fan-in 5) creates melody (V:1), the Harmonist (fan-in 5) writes harmony (V:2), and the Rhythmist (fan-in 5) produces bass (V:3) and drums (V:4). The Leader (fan-out 7) orchestrates iteration, the Reviewer evaluates quality across 6 dimensions, the Orchestrator plans expression (CC/pitch bend), and Revision performs minimal format fixes.

tour.title: Python Scripts & Expression Pipeline
tour.description: The Python toolchain that powers the composition pipeline. abc_to_midi converts ABC notation to MIDI, validate_abc runs 6 music-theory checks (key, range, leaps, duration, alignment, overlap), merge_abc combines multi-voice scores, and inject_expression adds CC7/CC10/CC91/pitch bend from an expression_plan.json. clef_tools.py provides the unified CLI entry point.
